title: Direct Gravimetric Determination of Copper(II) with a New Reagent, 3-( o-Carboxyphenyl)-I-o-tolyl-triazene-N-oxide
author: N. M. BANERJEE
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
Department of Chemistry, Jadavpur University, Calcutta-700 032 

 Manuscript received 20 June 1983, revised 24 February 1984, accepted 17 June 1984 

 3-( o- Carboxyphenyl)-1- o -tolyl-triazene- N -oxide has been used for the gravimetric determination of 2-30 mg Cu  I I , The green complex, Cu(C 14 O 3 N 3 H 11  ) is precipitated in the pH range 3-9 adjusted with aqueous caustic soda and hydrochloric acid, and is weighed as such after drying at 110-120 o . Interferences caused by Zn  l l  , Cd  l l  , Mg   I I   , Ni   I I  , Co   l I  , MN   l l  . Cr   l l  , Fe   l l  , Bi   l l l  , Al   l I I  , Pb   I I  , Sn   l l  , As  I I I  ,Sb   l l l  , Mo  VI  , W  VI  , V   V  , Nb  v  , Ta  v  , Rh   l l   and Os  IV   could be overcome by adjustment of  p H and by using tartrate as the masking agent, while the effects of Fe   l l I   and Be   l l  , Hg   I I   and U   I V   are removed by ammonium bifluoride, potassium iodide, and ammonium acetate, respectively. Tartrate does not interfere while the effect of oxalate, citrate, F - , SCN - , P \(O^{-2}_4\) •, B \(O^{-3}_3\)  and As \(O^{-3}_3\)  could be removed simply by the control of  p H. The reagent has also been used in the determination of copper in alloys, The average relative error is ±0.50% and relative standard deviation is ±0.30.
